claims.evidence_text: The protein content of wheat may range from 7 to 22%, but mostly lies between 10 and 15%
claims.claim_summary: Wheat grain protein content ranges from 7 to 22%, most commonly 10-15%.

claims.evidence_text: The protein content of wheat may range from 7 to 22%, but mostly lies between 10 and 15%
claims.claim_summary: Wheat grain protein content typically lies between 10 and 15%.
Average protein content of common wheat grain is 10.6 g/100g.
claims.table_label: TABLE 1 | Average contents of grain constituents of common wheat

claims.evidence_text: Among environmental factors, nitrogen fertilization is the most prominent determinant linked to protein content and composition, but other factors such as soil fertility, precipitation, temperature, and altitude also play a role
claims.claim_summary: Nitrogen fertilization is the most prominent determinant of wheat grain protein content and composition.

claims.evidence_text: Dry (moisture content <12.5%), cool (<10◦C), and pest-free storage of wheat grains protects against crop failure by providing a buffer to ensure nutrition security worldwide.
claims.claim_summary: Safe storage of wheat grain requires moisture content below 12.5% and temperatures below 10°C.
Mature common wheat grain has an average water (moisture) content of about 12.7%.
claims.table_label: TABLE 1 | Average contents of grain constituents of common wheat
